programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: Another episode of the Heritage Chart - new music by heritage acts! With Mike Read.

From 1982 to 1987 the Superstore doors opened to celebrities and pop stars and it was all LIVE! General Manager Mike Read looks back at 5 years of Saturday morning TV mayhem.

UK Eurovision winners in 1981, but then went onto to have another six top 10 hits in the 80s. Here are their greatest!

Two songs from each year of the '80s - all of them Record Breakers. Cheryl has the stories of best-selling songs, record-breaking fundraisers and the world's richest recording deal!

If you thought video effects started with digital in the '90s, just look at these! Pushing the boundaries to make amazing animations - and some puppets!

An hour from the kings of 80s synth - Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark.
Who better to present a definitive countdown of 80s synthpop classics than OMD frontman Andy McCluskey? From Kraftwerk, Ultravox & Gary Numan to New Order, Yazoo and Pet Shop Boys.

programmes.show_name: Make Mine A Double with Pat Sharp!
programmes.show_description: They say 2 heads are better than one & in the 80s the charts were full of collaborations. So pour a large one & have double the fun with Pat Sharp's top 40 duets.

In order - four hours of the biggest karaoke anthems of the '80s, from Kool & The Gang and Kim Wilde to Donna Summer and Boy Meets Girl. Prepare to sing out loud!

A whole hour of videos filmed on a beach, by the coast or at the seaside! Can you spot all the locations? There's Sydney, Ibiza and Malibu to find.
Twelve salty videos filmed on the high seas, along the coast or by the seaside!

programmes.show_name: The Opening Track from Every NOW!
programmes.show_description: A look back at the opening tracks from all the '80s NOW albums - from Phil Collins on NOW 1 to Tears for Fears on NOW 16. Plus those TV adverts that made them famous!
